_meta.description: Single source of truth for all numbers in the TDA-LLM preprint. All 10 directions, final corrected values after Phase 5 audit.

sparsity_baseline.note: Perfect rank correlation between sparsity (top-10 PCA energy) and difficulty level

permutation_test.note: Terminal-layer topology is significantly more difficulty-sensitive than chance

D1_perlayer_zscore.caveat: Qwen-specific (1/4 models in D6). Non-Qwen models show early-layer peaks.

D2_correctness.note: Topological features predict correctness well above chance. AUROC=0.787 after alignment fix (was 0.580 with mismatched data). H0 persistence entropy is most predictive.

D3_spectral_vs_euclidean.result: Euclidean PH captures richer H1 structure than spectral PH across all layers
D3_spectral_vs_euclidean.note: Spectral PH near-zero at all non-embedding layers. Euclidean PH captures 25-47 H1 features per layer. Spectral Laplacian distances may smooth away loop structure in LLM hidden states.

D5_crocker.result: Monotonic difficulty gradient visible in CROCKER L1 distances
D5_crocker.note: Scale-dependent structure: difficulty gradient strongest at intermediate filtration scales. L3/L4 cluster together.

D6_cross_model.note: Level 1 minimum is universal (4/4 models). Non-monotonicity universal (4/4). Terminal-layer effect Qwen-specific (1/4). Aligned Qwen shows near-monotonic increase (r=0.9). StableLM L1 entropy=0 (only 1 H1 feature).

D7_intrinsic_dim.note: Intrinsic dimensionality increases monotonically with difficulty at terminal layer (6.67 to 12.01). Computed from original Qwen data (D7 is internal-only, SAFE category).

D8_token_topology.result: Problem region carries strong difficulty-dependent topological signal; instruction regions do not
D8_token_topology.note: H1 persistence entropy of problem-token point clouds increases with difficulty (delta=0.82). Instruction tokens show negligible signal (<0.04). Confirms topological signature is problem-content-specific.

D9_compression.note: Terminal layer (28) shows zero H1/H2 topological features across all difficulty levels. Supports Fay et al. compression hypothesis over Datta et al. resistance.

D10_binding.interpretation: Population-level topological similarity between attention and hidden-state spaces decreases with difficulty
D10_binding.caveat: Shuffle test shows trend is driven by level-level differences (population signal), not per-problem coupling. Per-problem matching adds additional signal (L1: 0.740 aligned vs 0.683 mismatched).
D10_binding.note: Binding decreases with difficulty (r=-0.9, p=0.037). Non-monotonic at L2/L3 (L3>L2). Original perfect monotonic decrease (r=-1.0) was coincidental artifact of mismatched data.
